title: Drinking Late at Night After a Snowfall in Thimphu
content:
The moon looms dangerously close tonight
like a lighthouse on the November shore
toward which willows unleaf themselves
seeking their innards.
We are at a bar deciding what it means to be still.
Temple bells trill on & on, tapering out
like thoughts breaking down at the mind's edge.
As those involving origins. To say dirt comes
from non-dirt, or not this, not that
in a language of inward regress. Mind,
like electricity, seeks a terminus &, finding none,
zeroes down. The bell ringer listened to the trill
to the cease, & ceased himself. That's one
way to home—reaching into yourself
until the search leads to great foreign
cities hurling at tremendous speed, cities
where each inhabitant opens a door into
inner cities. I know this street I can walk
back blind. It enters, & only a part
of it emerges out of a long mirror, & there,
I've greeted the dead in a possible language
as if they've moved houses one street
over, outside the possibility of here & now,
into a long happy retirement.
In the afternoon, they come out into the patio
to drink their cool tea inevitable as the other
half of the half moon.
excerpt: The moon looms dangerously close tonight / like a lighthouse on the November shore / toward which willows unleaf themselves / seeking their innards.
